duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: release() fails to increment userTotalStaked after restoring slashed funds to stake.amount, creating permanent accounting desynchronization that causes withdrawal underflow reverts and permanent fund lockup
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All ten threats describe the identical code defect: release() modifies stake.amount but omits userTotalStaked update. ECON-H011 (cross-stake contamination), ECON-H012/ECON2-H011 (forced perpetual staking via extend) are downstream consequences of the same root cause — the userTotalStaked deficit created by release(). ECON-H001 selected as representative for its specific line references (343-380), detailed proof_of_signal comparing with slash(), and comprehensive impact/economics analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: uint88 intermediate multiplication overflow in slash() calculation (percent * stake.amount) / 100 causes revert for large stakes, granting slashing immunity to whales
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All ten threats describe the identical arithmetic defect: uint88 × uint88 multiplication overflows for stakes >~3.09M GTC at 100% slash. ECON-H002 selected for CRITICAL rating, precise line references (277-278, 303), specific overflow threshold calculations, and comprehensive economic impact analysis of how this defeats Sybil resistance.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: withdrawSelfStake() missing amount > 0 validation check, unlike withdrawCommunityStake()
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the identical missing validation: withdrawSelfStake lacks the zero-amount check present in withdrawCommunityStake. TECH-H003 selected for more detailed parallel code comparison in investigation_rationale.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: No deduplication of address arrays in slash() allows compounding slashes beyond intended percentage when duplicates are present
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five threats describe the identical defect: slash() iterates arrays without deduplication, causing each duplicate entry to re-slash the remaining balance (compounding effect). TECH-H004 selected for clear proof_of_signal and concise high-impact description.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Missing __gap storage variable in upgradeable IdentityStaking contract risks storage slot collision on future UUPS upgrades
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the identical missing __gap pattern in the UUPS upgradeable contract. ECON-H005 selected for its comprehensive proof_of_signal covering the full contract scope and detailed potential_victim_impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: lockAndBurn() does not zero totalSlashed[roundToBurn] after transferring tokens, leaving stale accounting data in storage
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four describe the identical stale data issue: totalSlashed mapping retains value after burn transfer. ECON-H003 selected for specific line references (323-338), UUPS upgrade latent risk analysis, and detailed impact assessment.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Integer truncation in slash() allows micro-stakes to evade slashing when (percent * amount) / 100 rounds to zero, while still corrupting slashedInRound metadata
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the identical truncation defect for small stakes. TECH-H007 selected because it uniquely covers both the slashing evasion AND the metadata corruption aspect (slashedInRound updated despite zero slash), plus the downstream impact on round migration logic.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: burnAddress not validated in initialize() — address(0) or invalid address permanently bricks lockAndBurn and round progression
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five describe the identical missing validation: initialize() does not check _burnAddress for address(0), which bricks lockAndBurn if GTC reverts on zero-address transfers. ECON-H004 selected for HIGH rating, detailed config_evidence, and comprehensive impact analysis including the cascade effect on appeals and round progression.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GTC.sol test mock transferFrom() has allowance check removed, enabling unauthorized token theft if deployed as production token
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four describe the identical removed allowance check in GTC.sol transferFrom. ECON-H006 selected for CRITICAL rating with detailed proof_of_signal (lines 168-174), comprehensive attack economics, and clear config_evidence noting test-only designation.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GTC.sol test mock has mintingAllowedAfter time check commented out, allowing unlimited minting frequency
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the identical commented-out time check in GTC.sol mint(). ECON-H007 selected for more detailed economic impact analysis including compounding supply growth calculation.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Staking functions credit internal balances by input amount without verifying actual tokens received, causing insolvency with fee-on-transfer or non-standard ERC20 tokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four describe the identical accounting pattern: selfStake/communityStake credit stake.amount by the input amount without balance-delta verification or SafeERC20 usage. TECH-H013 frames it as 'phantom stake' from malicious token, others focus on fee-on-transfer — same root cause (no actual-received-amount check). ECON-H015 selected for clearest economic impact framing. Note: The TECH2-H003 removed here is the fee-on-transfer variant; the other TECH2-H003 (UUPS initialization) is a separate unique threat retained.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permissionless lockAndBurn can be called by anyone at the 90-day mark, front-running off-chain appeal processes and permanently destroying funds before release() is executed
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the identical race condition between permissionless lockAndBurn and the off-chain appeal/release process. ECON-H014 selected for more detailed economic analysis and clearer description of the off-chain governance timing mismatch.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Self-stake and community-stake uint88 accumulators can overflow via repeated staking, bricking user operations
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the identical pattern: uint88 accumulator (stake.amount and userTotalStaked) overflow from repeated staking without cap checks. OPS-H012 (selfStake) and OPS-H013 (communityStake) are the same vulnerability in parallel functions. OPS-H012 selected as it covers the core pattern.
